subsections.text: In this chapter:
“Board” means a district’s board of directors.
“Bonds” means:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: bonds;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: notes, including bond anticipation notes, revenue anticipation notes, and grant anticipation notes;

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: warrants;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: certificates of obligation;

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: interest-bearing contracts;

subsections.label: F
subsections.text: interest-bearing leases of property;

subsections.label: G
subsections.text: equipment trust certificates;

subsections.label: H
subsections.text: commercial paper; and

subsections.label: I
subsections.text: any obligation issued to refund any type of bond.
“Director” means a board member.

“District” means a rural rail transportation district created under this chapter or under Chapter 623 (Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles) , Acts of the 67th Legislature, Regular Session, 1981 (Article 6550c, Vernon’s Texas Civil Statutes), as that chapter existed before April 1, 2011.

subsections.label: 4-a
subsections.text: “Intelligent transportation system” means:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: innovative or intelligent technological transportation systems, infrastructure, or facilities, including elevated freight transportation facilities:

subsections.label: i
subsections.text: in proximity to, or within, an existing right-of-way on the state highway system; or

subsections.label: ii
subsections.text: that connect land ports of entry to the state highway system;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: communications or information processing systems that improve the efficiency, security, or safety of freight movement on the state highway system, including improving the conveyance of freight on dedicated intelligent freight lanes; or

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: a transportation facility or system that increases truck freight efficiencies in the boundaries of an intermodal facility or hub.
“Maintenance facility” includes a workshop, a service, storage, security, or personnel facility, temporary or transient lodging for district employees, and equipment for any type of facility.
“Maintenance and operating expenses” means all expenses of operating and maintaining a district and its rail facilities, including:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: all compensation, labor, materials, repairs, and extensions necessary, required, or convenient in the board’s discretion to render efficient service or to maintain and operate the district; and

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: taxes or other amounts paid, payable, or to be paid to the United States under Section 148(f), Internal Revenue Code of 1986, or any similar law.
“Rail facilities” means:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: passenger or freight rail facilities, including:

subsections.label: i
subsections.text: tracks;

subsections.label: ii
subsections.text: a rail line;

subsections.label: iii
subsections.text: switching, signaling, or other operating equipment;

subsections.label: iv
subsections.text: a depot;

subsections.label: v
subsections.text: a locomotive;

subsections.label: vi
subsections.text: rolling stock;

subsections.label: vii
subsections.text: a maintenance facility; and

subsections.label: viii
subsections.text: other real and personal property associated with a rail operation;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: an intermodal hub;

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: an automated conveyor belt for the movement of freight;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: an intelligent transportation system that operates with or as part of facilities described by Paragraphs (A)-(C); or

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: a system of facilities described by Paragraphs (A)-(D).
“Revenue” means the income, receipts, and collections received by, to be received by, or pledged to the district from or by any source, except a restricted gift or a grant in aid of construction.
“Right-of-way” means:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: a right of passage over property;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: a strip of land in length and width determined required, necessary, or convenient by the board over, on, or under which trackwork is or is to be constructed or acquired; or

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: a right of precedential passing.
“Rolling stock” means a locomotive, an engine, a rail car, a repair construction car, or another car designed to operate on trackwork.
“Trackwork” means track, a track bed, track bed preparation, a tie, a rail fastener, a slab, a rail, an emergency crossover, a setout track, storage track, and a switch.

history.raw: Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 786 (H.B.2557), Sec. 1, eff. June 15, 2017.
